For pŌüó(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)>pŌüó(­ØÆø|E)­ØæØconditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖp(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)>p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)italic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) > italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) to hold, it is sufficient that

1ŌłÆnŌüóŽĄŌēźŽĄ,1­Øæøitalic-ŽĄitalic-ŽĄ1-n\epsilon\geq\epsilon,1 - italic_n italic_ŽĄ Ōēź italic_ŽĄ ,

which simplifies to 1Ōēź(n+1)ŌüóŽĄ1­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ1\geq(n+1)\epsilon1 Ōēź ( italic_n + 1 ) italic_ŽĄ, or ŽĄŌēż1n+1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø1\epsilon\leq\frac{1}{n+1}italic_ŽĄ Ōēż divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG. The theorem assumes (n+1)ŌüóŽĄ<1­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ1(n+1)\epsilon<1( italic_n + 1 ) italic_ŽĄ < 1, which is exactly this condition. The strict inequalities pŌüó(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)Ōēź1ŌłÆŌłæŽĄjŌĆ▓>1ŌłÆnŌüóŽĄ­ØæØconditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ1subscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚ1­Øæøitalic-ŽĄp(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)\geq 1-\sum\epsilon^{\prime}_{j}>1-n\epsilonitalic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) Ōēź 1 - Ōłæ italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT > 1 - italic_n italic_ŽĄ and pŌüó(­ØÆø|E)ŌēżŽĄkŌĆ▓<ŽĄ­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­Øæśitalic-ŽĄp(\boldsymbol{z}|E)\leq\epsilon^{\prime}_{k}<\epsilonitalic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) Ōēż italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_ŽĄ ensure that pŌüó(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)>pŌüó(­ØÆø|E)­ØæØconditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖp(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)>p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)italic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) > italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ). Thus,

argmax­ØÆøpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)=­ØÆÖŌłŚ.subscriptargmax­ØÆø­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖsuperscript­ØÆÖ\operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)=% \boldsymbol{x}^{*}.roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) = bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT .

Combined with the argmax of q­Øæ×qitalic_q, this proves the main statement of Part 1:

argmax­ØÆøpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)=argmax­ØÆøqŌüó(­ØÆø|E)=­ØÆÖŌłŚ.subscriptargmax­ØÆø­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖsubscriptargmax­ØÆø­Øæ×conditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖsuperscript­ØÆÖ\operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)=% \operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}q(\boldsymbol{z}|E)=% \boldsymbol{x}^{*}.roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) = roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) = bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT .

Step 3: Tightness of the bound 1n+11­Øæø1\frac{1}{n+1}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG.

The bound ŽĄŌēż1n+1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø1\epsilon\leq\frac{1}{n+1}italic_ŽĄ Ōēż divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG is tight. This means if ŽĄ>1n+1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø1\epsilon>\frac{1}{n+1}italic_ŽĄ > divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG, one can construct a scenario where the marginal conditions pjŌüó(Xij=xij|E)>1ŌłÆŽĄsubscript­ØæØ­ØæŚsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚconditionalsubscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚ­ØÉĖ1italic-ŽĄp_{j}(X_{i_{j}}=x_{i_{j}}|E)>1-\epsilonitalic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) > 1 - italic_ŽĄ hold, but argmax­ØÆøpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)ŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚsubscriptargmax­ØÆø­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖsuperscript­ØÆÖ\operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)\neq% \boldsymbol{x}^{*}roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT (which is argmax­ØÆøqŌüó(­ØÆø|E)subscriptargmax­ØÆø­Øæ×conditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ\operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}q(\boldsymbol{z}|E)roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) as long as ŽĄŌēż1/2italic-ŽĄ12\epsilon\leq 1/2italic_ŽĄ Ōēż 1 / 2).

Consider a vocabulary ­ØÆ▒={0,1}­ØÆ▒01\mathcal{V}=\{0,1\}caligraphic_V = { 0 , 1 } and let xij=0subscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚ0x_{i_{j}}=0italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 0 for all j­ØæŚjitalic_j, so ­ØÆÖŌłŚ=(0,ŌĆ”,0)superscript­ØÆÖ0ŌĆ”0\boldsymbol{x}^{*}=(0,\dots,0)bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( 0 , ŌĆ” , 0 ). For each jŌłł{1,ŌĆ”,n}­ØæŚ1ŌĆ”­Øæøj\in\{1,\dots,n\}italic_j Ōłł { 1 , ŌĆ” , italic_n }, let ­ØÉ×jsubscript­ØÉ×­ØæŚ\mathbf{e}_{j}bold_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the vector with 1111 at position j­ØæŚjitalic_j and 00 elsewhere. Let ╬Ę=1n+1Ōüó(ŽĄŌłÆ1n+1)>0­Ø£é1­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø10\eta=\frac{1}{n+1}(\epsilon-\frac{1}{n+1})>0italic_╬Ę = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG ( italic_ŽĄ - divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG ) > 0. Set pŌüó(­ØÉ×j|E)=1n+1+1nŌüó╬Ę,ŌłĆ1ŌēżjŌēżnformulae-sequence­ØæØconditionalsubscript­ØÉ×­ØæŚ­ØÉĖ1­Øæø11­Øæø­Ø£éfor-all1­ØæŚ­Øæøp(\mathbf{e}_{j}|E)=\frac{1}{n+1}+\frac{1}{n}\eta,\ \forall 1\leq j\leq nitalic_p ( bold_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG + divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n end_ARG italic_╬Ę , ŌłĆ 1 Ōēż italic_j Ōēż italic_n and pŌüó(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)=1n+1ŌłÆ╬Ę­ØæØconditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ1­Øæø1­Ø£ép(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)=\frac{1}{n+1}-\etaitalic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG - italic_╬Ę , then ­ØÆÖŌłŚŌłēargmax­ØÆøpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)superscript­ØÆÖsubscriptargmax­ØÆø­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ\boldsymbol{x}^{*}\notin\operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}p(% \boldsymbol{z}|E)bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT Ōłē roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ). The marginal probabilities are:

pjŌüó(Xij=1|E)subscript­ØæØ­ØæŚsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚconditional1­ØÉĖ\displaystyle p_{j}(X_{i_{j}}=1|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 1 | italic_E ) =pŌüó(­ØÉ×j|E)=1n+1+1nŌüó╬Ę,ŌłĆ1ŌēżjŌēżn.formulae-sequenceabsent­ØæØconditionalsubscript­ØÉ×­ØæŚ­ØÉĖ1­Øæø11­Øæø­Ø£éfor-all1­ØæŚ­Øæø\displaystyle=p(\mathbf{e}_{j}|E)=\frac{1}{n+1}+\frac{1}{n}\eta,\ \forall 1% \leq j\leq n.= italic_p ( bold_e start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG + divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n end_ARG italic_╬Ę , ŌłĆ 1 Ōēż italic_j Ōēż italic_n .
pjŌüó(Xij=0|E)subscript­ØæØ­ØæŚsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚconditional0­ØÉĖ\displaystyle p_{j}(X_{i_{j}}=0|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 0 | italic_E ) =1ŌłÆpjŌüó(Xij=1|E)=1ŌłÆŽĄc=nn+1ŌłÆ1nŌüó╬Ę>1ŌłÆŽĄ,absent1subscript­ØæØ­ØæŚsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚconditional1­ØÉĖ1subscriptitalic-ŽĄ­ØæÉ­Øæø­Øæø11­Øæø­Ø£é1italic-ŽĄ\displaystyle=1-p_{j}(X_{i_{j}}=1|E)=1-\epsilon_{c}=\frac{n}{n+1}-\frac{1}{n}% \eta>1-\epsilon,= 1 - italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 1 | italic_E ) = 1 - italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_c end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = divide start_ARG italic_n end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG - divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n end_ARG italic_╬Ę > 1 - italic_ŽĄ ,

because

1nŌüó╬Ę=1nŌüó(n+1)Ōüó(ŽĄŌłÆ1n+1)<ŽĄŌłÆ1n+11­Øæø­Ø£é1­Øæø­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø1\frac{1}{n}\eta=\frac{1}{n(n+1)}(\epsilon-\frac{1}{n+1})<\epsilon-\frac{1}{n+1}divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n end_ARG italic_╬Ę = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n ( italic_n + 1 ) end_ARG ( italic_ŽĄ - divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG ) < italic_ŽĄ - divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG

So, the marginal condition pjŌüó(Xij=xij|E)>1ŌłÆŽĄsubscript­ØæØ­ØæŚsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚconditionalsubscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚ­ØÉĖ1italic-ŽĄp_{j}(X_{i_{j}}=x_{i_{j}}|E)>1-\epsilonitalic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) > 1 - italic_ŽĄ (with xij=0subscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚ0x_{i_{j}}=0italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = 0) holds. As shown, argmax­ØÆøpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)subscriptargmax­ØÆø­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ\operatornamewithlimits{argmax}_{\boldsymbol{z}}p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)roman_argmax start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) can be made different from ­ØÆÖŌłŚsuperscript­ØÆÖ\boldsymbol{x}^{*}bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT. Thus, if ŽĄ>1n+1italic-ŽĄ1­Øæø1\epsilon>\frac{1}{n+1}italic_ŽĄ > divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG italic_n + 1 end_ARG, the argmax of p­ØæØpitalic_p and q­Øæ×qitalic_q may not be the same.

Step 4: Bound the Lpsubscript­ØÉ┐­ØæØL_{p}italic_L start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT distance. Let Ajsubscript­ØÉ┤­ØæŚA_{j}italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT be the event {Xij=xij}subscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚsubscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚ\{X_{i_{j}}=x_{i_{j}}\}{ italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT }.

Dp(p,q)p=|p(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)|p+Ōłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|p.D_{p}\left(p,q\right)^{p}=|p(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)-q(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)|^{p% }+\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)-q(% \boldsymbol{z}|E)|^{p}.italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p , italic_q ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = | italic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) | start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT .

The term |p(Ōł®j=1nAj|E)ŌłÆŌłÅj=1np(Aj|E)||p(\cap_{j=1}^{n}A_{j}|E)-\prod_{j=1}^{n}p(A_{j}|E)|| italic_p ( Ōł® start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) - ŌłÅ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p ( italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) | (using pŌüó(Aj|E)­ØæØconditionalsubscript­ØÉ┤­ØæŚ­ØÉĖp(A_{j}|E)italic_p ( italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) for pjŌüó(Xij=xij|E)subscript­ØæØ­ØæŚsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚconditionalsubscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­ØæŚ­ØÉĖp_{j}(X_{i_{j}}=x_{i_{j}}|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E )) can be bounded. Since

1ŌłÆŌłæj=1nŽĄjŌĆ▓ŌēżpŌüó(Ōł®j=1nAj|E)Ōēżmin1ŌēżjŌēżnŌüĪpŌüó(Aj|E)=1ŌłÆmax1ŌēżjŌēżnŌüĪŽĄjŌĆ▓,1superscriptsubscript­ØæŚ1­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚ­ØæØsuperscriptsubscript­ØæŚ1­Øæøconditionalsubscript­ØÉ┤­ØæŚ­ØÉĖsubscript1­ØæŚ­Øæø­ØæØconditionalsubscript­ØÉ┤­ØæŚ­ØÉĖ1subscript1­ØæŚ­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚ1-\sum_{j=1}^{n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{j}\leq p(\cap_{j=1}^{n}A_{j}|E)\leq\min_{1% \leq j\leq n}p(A_{j}|E)=1-\max_{1\leq j\leq n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{j},1 - Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT Ōēż italic_p ( Ōł® start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) Ōēż roman_min start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 Ōēż italic_j Ōēż italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) = 1 - roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 Ōēż italic_j Ōēż italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ,
1ŌłÆŌłæj=1nŽĄjŌĆ▓ŌēżŌłÅj=1n(1ŌłÆŽĄjŌĆ▓)=ŌłÅj=1npŌüó(Aj|E)Ōēż1ŌłÆmax1ŌēżjŌēżnŌüĪŽĄjŌĆ▓.1superscriptsubscript­ØæŚ1­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚsuperscriptsubscriptproduct­ØæŚ1­Øæø1subscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚsuperscriptsubscriptproduct­ØæŚ1­Øæø­ØæØconditionalsubscript­ØÉ┤­ØæŚ­ØÉĖ1subscript1­ØæŚ­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚ1-\sum_{j=1}^{n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{j}\leq\prod_{j=1}^{n}(1-\epsilon^{\prime}_{% j})=\prod_{j=1}^{n}p(A_{j}|E)\leq 1-\max_{1\leq j\leq n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{j}.1 - Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT Ōēż ŌłÅ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 - italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = ŌłÅ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p ( italic_A start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) Ōēż 1 - roman_max start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 Ōēż italic_j Ōēż italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT .

Thus,

|p(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)|<(nŌłÆ1)ŽĄ.|p(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)-q(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)|<(n-1)\epsilon.| italic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) | < ( italic_n - 1 ) italic_ŽĄ .

For ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ­ØÆøsuperscript­ØÆÖ\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT: pŌüó(­ØÆø|E)<ŽĄ­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖitalic-ŽĄp(\boldsymbol{z}|E)<\epsilonitalic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) < italic_ŽĄ and qŌüó(­ØÆø|E)<ŽĄ­Øæ×conditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖitalic-ŽĄq(\boldsymbol{z}|E)<\epsilonitalic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) < italic_ŽĄ. So,

|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|<ŽĄ.|p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)-q(\boldsymbol{z}|E)|<\epsilon.| italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | < italic_ŽĄ .

The sum Ōłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)-q(\boldsymbol{% z}|E)|Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | can be bounded:

Ōłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|\displaystyle\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)-q% (\boldsymbol{z}|E)|Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | ŌēżŌłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ(pŌüó(­ØÆø|E)+qŌüó(­ØÆø|E))=pŌüó(­Øæ┐ŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)+qŌüó(­Øæ┐ŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E).absentsubscript­ØÆøsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ­Øæ×conditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ­ØæØ­Øæ┐conditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ­Øæ×­Øæ┐conditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ\displaystyle\leq\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}(p(\boldsymbol{z}|% E)+q(\boldsymbol{z}|E))=p(\boldsymbol{X}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)+q(% \boldsymbol{X}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E).Ōēż Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) + italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) ) = italic_p ( bold_italic_X ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) + italic_q ( bold_italic_X ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) .
pŌüó(­Øæ┐ŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)­ØæØ­Øæ┐conditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ\displaystyle p(\boldsymbol{X}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)italic_p ( bold_italic_X ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) =1ŌłÆpŌüó(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)<1ŌłÆ(1ŌłÆŌłæj=1nŽĄjŌĆ▓)=Ōłæj=1nŽĄjŌĆ▓<nŌüóŽĄ.absent1­ØæØconditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ11superscriptsubscript­ØæŚ1­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚsuperscriptsubscript­ØæŚ1­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚ­Øæøitalic-ŽĄ\displaystyle=1-p(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)<1-(1-\sum_{j=1}^{n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{% j})=\sum_{j=1}^{n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{j}<n\epsilon.= 1 - italic_p ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) < 1 - ( 1 - Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) = Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_n italic_ŽĄ .
qŌüó(­Øæ┐ŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)­Øæ×­Øæ┐conditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ\displaystyle q(\boldsymbol{X}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)italic_q ( bold_italic_X ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) =1ŌłÆqŌüó(­ØÆÖŌłŚ|E)<1ŌłÆŌłÅj=1n(1ŌłÆŽĄjŌĆ▓)ŌēżŌłæj=1nŽĄjŌĆ▓<nŌüóŽĄ.absent1­Øæ×conditionalsuperscript­ØÆÖ­ØÉĖ1superscriptsubscriptproduct­ØæŚ1­Øæø1subscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚsuperscriptsubscript­ØæŚ1­Øæøsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­ØæŚ­Øæøitalic-ŽĄ\displaystyle=1-q(\boldsymbol{x}^{*}|E)<1-\prod_{j=1}^{n}(1-\epsilon^{\prime}_% {j})\leq\sum_{j=1}^{n}\epsilon^{\prime}_{j}<n\epsilon.= 1 - italic_q ( bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT | italic_E ) < 1 - ŌłÅ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 1 - italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) Ōēż Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j = 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_n italic_ŽĄ .

So,

Ōłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|<2nŽĄ.\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)-q(\boldsymbol{% z}|E)|<2n\epsilon.Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | < 2 italic_n italic_ŽĄ .

Then,

Ōłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|p\displaystyle\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)-q% (\boldsymbol{z}|E)|^{p}Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT Ōēż(sup­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|)pŌłÆ1Ōłæ­ØÆøŌēĀ­ØÆÖŌłŚ|p(­ØÆø|E)ŌłÆq(­ØÆø|E)|\displaystyle\leq(\sup_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(\boldsymbol{z}% |E)-q(\boldsymbol{z}|E)|)^{p-1}\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}\neq\boldsymbol{x}^{*}}|p(% \boldsymbol{z}|E)-q(\boldsymbol{z}|E)|Ōēż ( roman_sup start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) | ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p - 1 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z ŌēĀ bold_italic_x start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌłŚ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) - italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) |
<ŽĄpŌłÆ1Ōüó(2ŌüónŌüóŽĄ)=2ŌüónŌüóŽĄp.absentsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄ­ØæØ12­Øæøitalic-ŽĄ2­Øæøsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄ­ØæØ\displaystyle<\epsilon^{p-1}(2n\epsilon)=2n\epsilon^{p}.< italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p - 1 end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ( 2 italic_n italic_ŽĄ ) = 2 italic_n italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT .

Therefore,

DpŌüó(p,q)p<((nŌłÆ1)ŌüóŽĄ)p+2ŌüónŌüóŽĄp=((nŌłÆ1)p+2Ōüón)ŌüóŽĄp.subscript­ØÉĘ­ØæØsuperscript­ØæØ­Øæ×­ØæØsuperscript­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ­ØæØ2­Øæøsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄ­ØæØsuperscript­Øæø1­ØæØ2­Øæøsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄ­ØæØD_{p}\left(p,q\right)^{p}<((n-1)\epsilon)^{p}+2n\epsilon^{p}=((n-1)^{p}+2n)% \epsilon^{p}.italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p , italic_q ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT < ( ( italic_n - 1 ) italic_ŽĄ ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + 2 italic_n italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT = ( ( italic_n - 1 ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + 2 italic_n ) italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT .

So,

DpŌüó(p,q)<((nŌłÆ1)p+2Ōüón)1/pŌüóŽĄ.subscript­ØÉĘ­ØæØ­ØæØ­Øæ×superscriptsuperscript­Øæø1­ØæØ2­Øæø1­ØæØitalic-ŽĄD_{p}\left(p,q\right)<((n-1)^{p}+2n)^{1/p}\epsilon.italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p , italic_q ) < ( ( italic_n - 1 ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT + 2 italic_n ) start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT 1 / italic_p end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_ŽĄ .

For p=1­ØæØ1p=1italic_p = 1,

D1Ōüó(p,q)<(nŌłÆ1+2Ōüón)ŌüóŽĄ=(3ŌüónŌłÆ1)ŌüóŽĄ.subscript­ØÉĘ1­ØæØ­Øæ×­Øæø12­Øæøitalic-ŽĄ3­Øæø1italic-ŽĄD_{1}\left(p,q\right)<(n-1+2n)\epsilon=(3n-1)\epsilon.italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p , italic_q ) < ( italic_n - 1 + 2 italic_n ) italic_ŽĄ = ( 3 italic_n - 1 ) italic_ŽĄ .

And for Total Variation Distance,

DTŌüóVŌüó(p,q)=12ŌüóD1Ōüó(p,q)<3ŌüónŌłÆ12ŌüóŽĄ.subscript­ØÉĘ­Øæć­Øæē­ØæØ­Øæ×12subscript­ØÉĘ1­ØæØ­Øæ×3­Øæø12italic-ŽĄD_{TV}(p,q)=\frac{1}{2}D_{1}\left(p,q\right)<\frac{3n-1}{2}\epsilon.italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_T italic_V end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p , italic_q ) = divide start_ARG 1 end_ARG start_ARG 2 end_ARG italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p , italic_q ) < divide start_ARG 3 italic_n - 1 end_ARG start_ARG 2 end_ARG italic_ŽĄ .

Step 4: Bound the forward KL divergence.

DKLŌüó(pŌłźq)=Ōłæ­ØÆøpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)ŌüólogŌüĪpŌüó(­ØÆø|E)qŌüó(­ØÆø|E)=IŌüó(Xi1;ŌĆ”;Xin|E).subscript­ØÉĘKLconditional­ØæØ­Øæ×subscript­ØÆø­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ­ØæØconditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ­Øæ×conditional­ØÆø­ØÉĖ­ØÉ╝subscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢1ŌĆ”conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæø­ØÉĖD_{\mathrm{KL}}\left(p\|q\right)=\sum_{\boldsymbol{z}}p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)\log% \frac{p(\boldsymbol{z}|E)}{q(\boldsymbol{z}|E)}=I(X_{i_{1}};\dots;X_{i_{n}}|E).italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_KL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p Ōłź italic_q ) = Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_z end_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) roman_log divide start_ARG italic_p ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) end_ARG start_ARG italic_q ( bold_italic_z | italic_E ) end_ARG = italic_I ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ; ŌĆ” ; italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) .

The conditional total correlation can be expanded using the chain rule:

IŌüó(Xi1;ŌĆ”;Xin|E)=Ōłæk=2nIŌüó(Xik;Xi1,ŌĆ”,XikŌłÆ1|E).­ØÉ╝subscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢1ŌĆ”conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæø­ØÉĖsuperscriptsubscript­Øæś2­Øæø­ØÉ╝subscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæśsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢1ŌĆ”conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś1­ØÉĖI(X_{i_{1}};\dots;X_{i_{n}}|E)=\sum_{k=2}^{n}I(X_{i_{k}};X_{i_{1}},\dots,X_{i_% {k-1}}|E).italic_I ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ; ŌĆ” ; italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) = Ōłæ start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k = 2 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT italic_I ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ; italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , ŌĆ” , italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) .

Each term is bounded by the conditional entropy:

IŌüó(Xik;Xi1,ŌĆ”,XikŌłÆ1|E)ŌēżHŌüó(Xik|E).­ØÉ╝subscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæśsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢1ŌĆ”conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś1­ØÉĖ­ØÉ╗conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś­ØÉĖI(X_{i_{k}};X_{i_{1}},\dots,X_{i_{k-1}}|E)\leq H(X_{i_{k}}|E).italic_I ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ; italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , ŌĆ” , italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k - 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) Ōēż italic_H ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) .

The conditional entropy HŌüó(Xik|E)­ØÉ╗conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś­ØÉĖH(X_{i_{k}}|E)italic_H ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) is bounded. Since pkŌüó(Xik=xik|E)>1ŌłÆŽĄsubscript­ØæØ­Øæśsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæśconditionalsubscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś­ØÉĖ1italic-ŽĄp_{k}(X_{i_{k}}=x_{i_{k}}|E)>1-\epsilonitalic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT = italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) > 1 - italic_ŽĄ, it implies pkŌüó(XikŌēĀxik|E)=ŽĄkŌĆ▓<ŽĄsubscript­ØæØ­Øæśsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæśconditionalsubscript­Øæźsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś­ØÉĖsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­Øæśitalic-ŽĄp_{k}(X_{i_{k}}\neq x_{i_{k}}|E)=\epsilon^{\prime}_{k}<\epsilonitalic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ŌēĀ italic_x start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) = italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT < italic_ŽĄ. The entropy is maximized when the remaining probability ŽĄkŌĆ▓subscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­Øæś\epsilon^{\prime}_{k}italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT is spread uniformly, leading to:

HŌüó(Xik|E)ŌēżHbŌüó(ŽĄkŌĆ▓)+ŽĄkŌĆ▓ŌüólnŌüĪ(|­ØÆ▒|ŌłÆ1)<HbŌüó(ŽĄ)+ŽĄŌüólnŌüĪ(|­ØÆ▒|ŌłÆ1).­ØÉ╗conditionalsubscript­Øæŗsubscript­Øæ¢­Øæś­ØÉĖsubscript­ØÉ╗­ØæÅsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­Øæśsubscriptsuperscriptitalic-ŽĄŌĆ▓­Øæś­ØÆ▒1subscript­ØÉ╗­ØæÅitalic-ŽĄitalic-ŽĄ­ØÆ▒1H(X_{i_{k}}|E)\leq H_{b}(\epsilon^{\prime}_{k})+\epsilon^{\prime}_{k}\ln(|% \mathcal{V}|-1)<H_{b}(\epsilon)+\epsilon\ln(|\mathcal{V}|-1).italic_H ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) Ōēż italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ) + italic_ŽĄ start_POSTSUPERSCRIPT ŌĆ▓ end_POSTSUPERSCRIPT start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_ln ( | caligraphic_V | - 1 ) < italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_ŽĄ ) + italic_ŽĄ roman_ln ( | caligraphic_V | - 1 ) .

Summing (nŌłÆ1)­Øæø1(n-1)( italic_n - 1 ) such terms (for k=2,ŌĆ”,n­Øæś2ŌĆ”­Øæøk=2,\dots,nitalic_k = 2 , ŌĆ” , italic_n):

DKLŌüó(pŌłźq)<(nŌłÆ1)Ōüó[HbŌüó(ŽĄ)+ŽĄŌüólnŌüĪ(|­ØÆ▒|ŌłÆ1)].subscript­ØÉĘKLconditional­ØæØ­Øæ×­Øæø1delimited-[]subscript­ØÉ╗­ØæÅitalic-ŽĄitalic-ŽĄ­ØÆ▒1D_{\mathrm{KL}}\left(p\|q\right)<(n-1)[H_{b}(\epsilon)+\epsilon\ln(|\mathcal{V% }|-1)].italic_D start_POSTSUBSCRIPT roman_KL end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_p Ōłź italic_q ) < ( italic_n - 1 ) [ italic_H start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_b end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_ŽĄ ) + italic_ŽĄ roman_ln ( | caligraphic_V | - 1 ) ] .

ŌłÄ

Remark 1.

Assumption of a Well-Defined Joint p­Ø£ĮŌüó(Xi1,ŌĆ”,Xin|E)subscriptp­Ø£ĮsubscriptXsubscripti1ŌĆ”conditionalsubscriptXsubscriptinEp_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}(X_{i_{1}},\dots,X_{i_{n}}|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_╬Ė end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , ŌĆ” , italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ): The theorem and proof rely on p­ØøēŌüó(Xi1,ŌĆ”,Xin|E)subscriptp­ØøēsubscriptXsubscripti1ŌĆ”conditionalsubscriptXsubscriptinEp_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}(X_{i_{1}},\dots,X_{i_{n}}|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_╬Ė end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT 1 end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT , ŌĆ” , italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_n end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) being a well-defined joint probability mass function from which the marginals p­ØøēŌüó(Xij|E)subscriptp­ØøēconditionalsubscriptXsubscriptijEp_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}(X_{i_{j}}|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_╬Ė end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT | italic_E ) are consistently derived. This implies that the joint PMF is coherent and its definition does not depend on a specific factorization order beyond what is captured by the conditioning on EEEitalic_E. In practice, while MDM may not strictly satisfy this property, its behavior typically offers a close approximation. The theorem holds for an idealized p­Øøēsubscriptp­Øøēp_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_╬Ė end_POSTSUBSCRIPT that possesses these properties. As MDMs become larger and more powerful, their learned distributions might better approximate such consistency.

Worst-Case Analysis: The conditions and bounds provided in the theorem (e.g., (n+1)ŌüóŽĄŌēż1n1ŽĄ1(n+1)\epsilon\leq 1( italic_n + 1 ) italic_ŽĄ Ōēż 1) are derived from a worst-case analysis. This means the bounds are guaranteed to hold if the conditions are met, regardless of the specific structure of p­ØøēŌüó(­ØÉŚ|E)subscriptp­Øøēconditional­ØÉŚEp_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}(\boldsymbol{X}|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_╬Ė end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( bold_italic_X | italic_E ) beyond the high-confidence marginal property. In practice, the actual case might be "better behaved" than the worst-case scenario. For instance, the dependencies between XijsubscriptXsubscriptijX_{i_{j}}italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_j end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT and XiksubscriptXsubscriptikX_{i_{k}}italic_X start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_i start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_k end_POSTSUBSCRIPT end_POSTSUBSCRIPT (given EEEitalic_E) might be weaker than what the worst-case construction assumes. Consequently, the argmax equivalence (Result 1) might still hold frequently even if (n+1)ŌüóŽĄn1ŽĄ(n+1)\epsilon( italic_n + 1 ) italic_ŽĄ is slightly greater than 1 (but not much larger). The condition identifies a threshold beyond which guarantees break down in the worst case, but practical performance can be more robust. Similarly, the actual LpsubscriptLpL_{p}italic_L start_POSTSUBSCRIPT italic_p end_POSTSUBSCRIPT distances or KL divergence might be smaller than the upper bounds suggest if the true joint p­ØøēŌüó(­ØÉŚ|E)subscriptp­Øøēconditional­ØÉŚEp_{\boldsymbol{\theta}}(\boldsymbol{X}|E)italic_p start_POSTSUBSCRIPT bold_italic_╬Ė end_POSTSUBSCRIPT ( bold_italic_X | italic_E ) is closer to the product of marginals qŌüó(­ØÉŚ|E)qconditional­ØÉŚEq(\boldsymbol{X}|E)italic_q ( bold_italic_X | italic_E ) than the worst-case configurations.

Appendix B Case Study

B.1 Effect of Caching Strategies on Response Quality

Table 6 qualitatively compares answers from the Original, PrefixCache, and DualCache methods for the arithmetic prompt. All correctly compute the answer (3 bolts), following similar step-by-step reasoning, with only minor differences in phrasing. This shows cache strategies maintain answer accuracy and logical clarity while improving efficiency; semantic fidelity and interpretability are unaffected.

B.2 Effect of Block Size in DualCache

Table 7 examines different block sizes (8, 16, 32) in DualCache. For this arithmetic prompt, all settings yield correct, clearly explained answers with no meaningful output differences. Thus, DualCache is robust to block size for such problems, allowing efficiency improvements without compromising quality.

B.3 Impact of Dynamic Threshold Settings

Table 8 investigates dynamic threshold values (0.7, 0.8, 0.9). The model consistently produces the correct answer and clear explanations, regardless of threshold. While higher thresholds increase computational effort (NFE from 9 to 20), answer quality remains stable, indicating threshold adjustment mainly affects efficiency, not correctness, for straightforward arithmetic questions.

B.4 Multimodal Generation with LLAda-V

To qualitatively analyze the effectiveness of our Fast-dLLM framework in multimodal scenarios, we conduct a visual case study where the model is tasked with generating a highly detailed image description. As illustrated in Figure 6, both the baseline model and our Fast-dLLM are given the same visual input and user prompt: ŌĆ£Please describe the image in detail.ŌĆØ

The baseline model requires 63.0 seconds to complete the generation, producing a detailed and poetic description of the rural landscape. It highlights elements such as the weathered wooden barn, the soft pink sky, and the tranquil atmosphere.

In contrast, our Fast-dLLM completes the task in just 6.8 secondsŌĆöa nearly 10├Ś\times├Ś speedupŌĆöwhile maintaining rich visual detail. It further enhances the description with additional grounding (e.g., ŌĆ£gray shingles on its roofŌĆØ, ŌĆ£touch of tranquilityŌĆØ), reflecting a strong alignment with both appearance and mood cues from the image. Notably, the generated caption retains compositional depth and stylistic fluency, illustrating the modelŌĆÖs ability to balance fluency and factuality even under diffusion-based parallel decoding.

This case highlights how LLAda-V with Fast-dLLM decoding enables high-quality vision-language generation at significantly improved efficiency, paving the way for faster and more interactive multimodal applications.

Appendix C Experiment Details

C.1 Further Experiments with LLaDA-V

In Table 9, we investigate how the choice of block length affects the performance of LLaDA-V on MathVista under a fixed decoding length of 48 steps. The results show that the model achieves the highest accuracy with a block length of 96. However, when reducing the block size to 8 or 4, the accuracy drops significantly by over 8%.

Given this sensitivity to block length, we choose not to break the output into small blocks for updating caches individually. Instead, we keep the block length fixed at 96 and adopt a refresh-based strategy: the cache is updated only every r­Øæ¤ritalic_r decoding steps using the most recent full block. As shown in Table 10, increasing the refresh interval leads to consistent gains in throughputŌĆöfrom 15.9 tokens/s at interval 2 to 28.2 tokens/s at interval 32. While accuracy drops slightly with larger intervals, it remains above 56.6%, suggesting that aggressive refresh scheduling can yield substantial speedups with only minor performance degradation.

C.2 Performance Comparison between Threshold and Factor Strategy

We compare the performance of our threshold-based and factor-based confidence-aware parallel decoding strategies on GSM8K and MATH benchmarks (Table 11). While the threshold strategy achieves marginally better accuracy in most settings (e.g., 78.5% vs. 77.5% on GSM8K with 256 tokens), the factor strategy demonstrates substantially superior throughput performance.

Specifically, factor decoding achieves 1.4-1.5├Ś higher throughput than threshold decoding across all settings. On GSM8K with 256 tokens, factor decoding reaches 78.5 tokens/sec (11.7├Ś speedup) compared to 54.4 tokens/sec (8.1├Ś speedup) for threshold decoding. This throughput advantage becomes even more pronounced on longer generation tasksŌĆöfor GSM8K with 512 tokens, factor decoding attains 47.1 tokens/sec while threshold only achieves 35.3 tokens/sec.

The results demonstrate that factor decoding offers a compelling trade-off: it sacrifices minimal accuracy (typically 1-3%) in exchange for significant throughput improvements (40-50% higher). This makes factor decoding particularly attractive for latency-sensitive applications where the slight accuracy reduction is acceptable. The consistent pattern across both benchmarks and generation lengths validates the robustness of the factor strategyŌĆÖs theoretical foundation, which adaptively controls parallelism based on the confidence bound (n+1)ŌüóŽĄ<f­Øæø1italic-ŽĄ­Øæō(n+1)\epsilon<f( italic_n + 1 ) italic_ŽĄ < italic_f.

C.3 Comparison between LLaDA and LLaDA-1.5

We compare the performance of LLaDA and its enhanced version LLaDA-1.5 across both GSM8K (5-shot) and MATH (4-shot) benchmarks under two generation length settings (256 and 512 tokens), as shown in Table LABEL:tab:llada_1.5. Each cell reports accuracy and decoding throughput (in tokens per second), along with the relative speedup over the greedy baseline.

Across GSM8K settings, LLaDA-1.5 consistently improves accuracy over the original LLaDA, achieving a notable +2.2% absolute gain at 256-token generation and +3.2% at 512-token generation. Furthermore, it maintains strong decoding efficiency, with throughput reaching 59.4 tokens/sec at 256 tokens, improving upon LLaDAŌĆÖs 54.1 tokens/sec under the same setting.

On the MATH benchmark, accuracy between the two versions remains comparable. However, LLaDA-1.5 slightly improves throughput at 256 tokens (53.7 vs. 51.7) while incurring a mild efficiency regression at the 512-token setting (41.1 vs. 47.1). This suggests that while LLaDA-1.5 introduces enhancements beneficial for shorter or moderate decoding contexts, longer sequences may require further optimization.

Overall, LLaDA-1.5 consistently provides either superior accuracy or better decoding speed across settings, demonstrating better performance-efficiency trade-offs and highlighting the benefit of incorporating adaptive improvements on top of the base LLaDA architecture.

C.4 Analysis of Parallel Token Counts across Decoding Steps

To better understand the behavior of factor-based parallel generation, we analyze the average number of tokens generated at each decoding step. Specifically, we collect statistics from all intermediate steps of the sampling process and compute the average number of tokens generated in parallel per step. The results are visualized in Figure 7, along with a 95% confidence interval indicating cross-sample variability.

As shown in Figure 7, the average number of tokens generated in parallel gradually increases during the early to middle stages of decoding, peaking roughly between step 30 to step 60. After this peak, the parallelism tends to slightly decline toward the end of generation. This suggests that the model becomes more confident in generating outputs during the mid-decoding phase, allowing it to produce more tokens simultaneously. Toward the final steps, the decoding process tends to become more conservative, reducing the number of tokens produced at each step.

The shaded confidence interval reveals greater variance in later decoding steps, indicating instability and inconsistent generation behavior across samples. This is expected since tail-end decoding steps tend to handle only a few remaining tokens required to complete the output, and the number of remaining tokens could differ widely among different samples (e.g., due to early completion or padding).

These observations are important for understanding how decoding efficiency can be optimized: increasing parallelism during high-confidence phases (middle steps) offers computational savings, while conservative behavior near boundaries maintains quality.

C.5 Throughput Comparison under Varying Batch Sizes

All experiments are conducted on an NVIDIA A100 GPU, with the prefill length fixed to 256 tokens. The generation length is varied among 16, 32, and 64 tokens, and batch sizes range from 1 to 32. This setup reflects realistic deployment scenarios, allowing the evaluation of decoding efficiency under diverse conditions.

It should be noted that parallel decoding allows multiple tokens to be generated simultaneously affected by dummy input tokens. To ensure fairness, we focus solely on the acceleration provided by caching techniques.

PrefixCache is designed as an acceleration mechanism for LLaDA, a diffusion-based LLM, and successfully boosts the throughput significantly. Figure 9 shows that PrefixCache achieves consistent improvements across all batch sizes and generation lengths, making it particularly suited for scenarios with smaller generation lengths and larger batch sizes. For instance, with a generation length of 16 and batch size of 32, PrefixCache achieves a throughput of over 211 tokens/s, significantly outperforming the native LLaDA which reaches only 43 tokens/s, demonstrating nearly 5├Ś5\times5 ├Ś improvement.

While LLaDA exhibits limited scalability with increasing batch sizesŌĆöits throughput plateaus after batch size 8ŌĆöthis limitation is inherent to diffusion-based LLMs, which are compute-bound by nature. In contrast, LLaMA, an autoregressive (AR) model, benefits greatly from large batch sizes. As the batch size increases, LLaMA shifts from being memory-bound to compute-bound, allowing it to achieve high absolute throughput at larger batch settings.

These results highlight the practical advantages of PrefixCache in accelerating compute-bound diffusion models like LLaDA, especially for latency-critical and high-throughput applications. Furthermore, the scalability and efficiency provided by PrefixCache bridge the gap between diffusion-based LLMs and AR models like LLaMA, showcasing its importance for large-scale deployment settings.

Report GitHub Issue
Generated by L A T E xml

Instructions for reporting errors

We are continuing to improve HTML versions of papers, and your feedback helps enhance accessibility and mobile support. To report errors in the HTML that will help us improve conversion and rendering, choose any of the methods listed below:

  • Click the "Report Issue" button.
  • Open a report feedback form via keyboard, use "Ctrl + ?".
  • Make a text selection and click the "Report Issue for Selection" button near your cursor.
  • You can use Alt+Y to toggle on and Alt+Shift+Y to toggle off accessible reporting links at each section.

Our team has already identified the following issues. We appreciate your time reviewing and reporting rendering errors we may not have found yet. Your efforts will help us improve the HTML versions for all readers, because disability should not be a barrier to accessing research. Thank you for your continued support in championing open access for all.

Have a free development cycle? Help support accessibility at arXiv! Our collaborators at LaTeXML maintain a list of packages that need conversion, and welcome developer contributions.
